title: Binomial theorem
body:
In elementary algebra, the binomial theorem (or binomial expansion) describes the algebraic expansion of powers of a binomial. According to the theorem, the power ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        
          (
          x
          +
          y
          
            )
            
              n
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle \textstyle (x+y)^{n}}
  
‚Ā† expands into a polynomial with terms of the form ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        
          a
          
            x
            
              k
            
          
          
            y
            
              m
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle \textstyle ax^{k}y^{m}}
  
‚Ā†, where the exponents ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        k
      
    
    {\displaystyle k}
  
‚Ā† and ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        m
      
    
    {\displaystyle m}
  
‚Ā† are nonnegative integers satisfying ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        k
        +
        m
        =
        n
      
    
    {\displaystyle k+m=n}
  
‚Ā† and the coefficient ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        a
      
    
    {\displaystyle a}
  
‚Ā† of each term is a specific positive integer depending on ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        n
      
    
    {\displaystyle n}
  
‚Ā† and ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        k
      
    
    {\displaystyle k}
  
‚Ā†. For example, for ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        n
        =
        4
      
    
    {\displaystyle n=4}
  
‚Ā†,

  
    
      
        (
        x
        +
        y
        
          )
          
            4
          
        
        =
        
          x
          
            4
          
        
        +
        4
        
          x
          
            3
          
        
        y
        +
        6
        
          x
          
            2
          
        
        
          y
          
            2
          
        
        +
        4
        x
        
          y
          
            3
          
        
        +
        
          y
          
            4
          
        
        .
      
    
    {\displaystyle (x+y)^{4}=x^{4}+4x^{3}y+6x^{2}y^{2}+4xy^{3}+y^{4}.}
  

The coefficient ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        a
      
    
    {\displaystyle a}
  
‚Ā† in each term ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        
          a
          
            x
            
              k
            
          
          
            y
            
              m
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle \textstyle ax^{k}y^{m}}
  
‚Ā† is known as the binomial coefficient ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        
          
            
              
                (
              
              
                n
                k
              
              
                )
              
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle {\tbinom {n}{k}}}
  
‚Ā† or ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        
          
            
              
                (
              
              
                n
                m
              
              
                )
              
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle {\tbinom {n}{m}}}
  
‚Ā† (the two have the same value). These coefficients for varying ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        n
      
    
    {\displaystyle n}
  
‚Ā† and ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        k
      
    
    {\displaystyle k}
  
‚Ā† can be arranged to form Pascal's triangle. These numbers also occur in combinatorics, where ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        
          
            
              
                (
              
              
                n
                k
              
              
                )
              
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle {\tbinom {n}{k}}}
  
‚Ā† gives the number of different combinations (i.e. subsets) of ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        k
      
    
    {\displaystyle k}
  
‚Ā† elements that can be chosen from an ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        n
      
    
    {\displaystyle n}
  
‚Ā†-element set. Therefore ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        
          
            
              
                (
              
              
                n
                k
              
              
                )
              
            
          
        
      
    
    {\displaystyle {\tbinom {n}{k}}}
  
‚Ā† is usually pronounced as "‚Ā†
  
    
      
        n
      
    
    {\displaystyle n}
  
‚Ā† choose ‚Ā†
  
    
      
        k
      
    
    {\displaystyle k}
  
‚Ā†".
